accept: act create accepts --branch <ref>; auto-commit targets that ref
--push uses the named branch upstream when --branch is supplied
integration test: subagent on worktree branch X runs act create --branch X --push; op commit lands on X, not main
payload.description: A subagent running in a git worktree on branch X calls act create --push (or update --push, close --push). The auto-commit targets the worktree branch, but --push uses the branch tracking config which often points to origin/main. Net: act op commits land on origin/main ahead of the work commit. Real damage in aac-website dogfood, act-8808: three act create --push calls from a worktree subagent committed to origin/main, requiring cherry-pick recovery for the work commit. Fix: act commands that write ops accept --branch <ref> so the op auto-commit and push target a named branch independent of HEAD/tracking. Worktree dispatchers pass --branch <worktree-branch>; integrator workflow stays robust to CWD discipline. Source: docs/aac-website-dogfood-debrief.md §1, §6.
payload.title: cli: --branch <ref> on op commands decouples op-writing from current working tree
